title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Player Outcome
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 6722 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Evidence Ladder, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A debug Asteroids team handles case 8532 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Evidence Ladder, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: A passing unit test proves the game feels responsive and accessible.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-evidence-ladder-1djvxw" that uses case 4829 to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Ladder into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-evidence-ladder-1djvxw
# Competency: asteroids-evidence-ladder.
# Case 4829: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_evidence_ladder_1djvxw(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1djvxw = "1djvxw-wxvjd1-4829"
    assert evidence_variant_1djvxw
    evidence_axes_1djvxw = (
        "player1djvxw",
        "seed4829",
        "inputwxvjd1",
        "frame1djvxw4829",
        "state48291djvxw",
        "playtestwxvjd14829",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1djvxw)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.
